toolCalls.result:
Brochure

NVIDIA ENTERPRISE AI 
REFERENCE ARCHITECTURE 
WITH HPE

Transforming data centers with flexible, scalable,  
and powerful server GPU solutions

 Redefining AI infrastructure with HPE and NVIDIA 

Today’s organizations are redefining what’s possible with artificial 
intelligence (AI)—pushing boundaries in research, business, and innovation. 
At the center of this transformation stands the NVIDIA AI Computing by 
HPE portfolio, a collaboration that unlocks new potential in enterprise 
AI infrastructure. By fusing robust HPE compute, software, and storage 
platforms with the latest advances in NVIDIA GPUs and software in  
jointly-validated configurations, blueprints maker it easier to deploy reliable, 
scalable, and high-performance AI solutions.

This reference architecture (RA) overview offers an NVIDIA-validated solution 
that scales from 4-32 nodes. HPE tailors and optimizes solution components 
for distinct use cases, environments and operational scale. Inside HPE 
ProLiant servers, NVIDIA® RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs deliver 
an entry point into data center–scale AI, balancing balance performance, cost, 
and flexibility. 

The collaboration between HPE and NVIDIA means this solution design is 
technically robust and backed by a collaborative ecosystem of support, 
management, and future-ready innovation. The result is a foundation that 
speeds time to insight for AI projects of any size, and enables enterprises to 
meet the evolving demands of data analytics and artificial intelligence.

NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architecture with HPE   |  2

 Enterprise AI Reference Architecture with 
NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

Ideal use cases: This cluster architecture is designed for organizations 
seeking a flexible, scalable entry point into AI workloads at data center scale. 
It is particularly well-suited for AI inference and fine tuning for models less 
than 70B parameters, and a range of enterprise applications where modular 
expansion and balanced cost-performance are essential. The design is 
optimized for environments prioritizing rapid deployment, reliability, and 
efficient scaling, making it a strong fit for enterprise data centers launching 
new AI initiatives.

The NVIDIA Enterprise AI Reference Architecture (Enterprise RA) using 
RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs are built to solve today’s 
most challenging computational problems. This architecture employs a 
modular  design, with each HPE ProLiant Compute DL380a Gen12 server 
equipped with up to eight RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs and the NVIDIA 
Spectrum-X™ Networking Platform to power next-generation AI use cases.

Key components:

Architecture design:

 — HPE ProLiant servers, each with up to 8 RTX 
PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs

 — NVIDIA Spectrum-4 networking platform

 — NVIDIA Connect-X-7 SuperNICs and DPUs

 — NVIDIA AI Enterprise software

 — Composed of scalable units (SUs), each with 
4 HPE ProLiant servers, allowing for rapid and 
flexible deployment at multiple scales

 — Scales to 32 certified systems, for a total of  
256 RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs in a fully 
tested system (8 SUs)

 — Flexible, rail-optimized network architecture 

and support for custom rack layouts

Management and support:

Primary use cases:

 — NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA CUDA®, and 

NVIDIA Magnum IO™

 — Hardware support via HPE fulfillment, software 
support via NVIDIA AI Enterprise subscription

 — AI inference and fine-tuning for large models 
up to 70B parameters, powering copilots, 
assistants and agentic AI

 — Hybrid AI and single precision HPC workloads 
requiring modular, scalable infrastructure 
for high-performance data processing and 
simulation

 — Data analytics, visual computing and 

engineering simulation to accelerate insight, 
design, and decision-making

This Enterprise RA provides a robust and adaptable framework for 
organizations to innovate confidently, supported by the combined strengths 
of HPE and NVIDIA engineering, software, and support ecosystem.

NVIDIA Enterprise AI Reference Architecture with HPE   |  3

 Blueprints with NVIDIA: A platform for accelerated innovation

Reference architectures with NVIDIA represent a new era 
of enterprise-ready AI—designs to streamline AI 
adoption, accelerate deployment, and scale innovation 
across a wide range of industries and use cases. The 
resulting HPE AI solutions with NVIDIA provide a holistic 
environment where compute, software, storage, 
networking, and server GPU acceleration can be 
integrated to create an agile AI infrastructure.

Collaboration is another hallmark of the enterprise AI 
design. The partnership between HPE and NVIDIA brings 
together expertise in solution engineering. The 
installation and deployment expertise from HPE gives 
enterprise clients the confidence to deploy AI at scale. 
The design’s modular nature not only simplifies 
expansion, but also empowers organizations to integrate 
their AI workloads

with broader IT initiatives.

For industries ranging from healthcare and life sciences 
to finance, manufacturing, and retail, HPE enterprise AI 
solutions with NVIDIA enable faster time to insight,

Central to these architectures is a philosophy of

NVIDIA Enterprise AI Reference Architecture with HPE   |  4

 Why HPE for AI solutions

Many teams face familiar barriers, such as fragmented data, security 
concerns, and infrastructure that can’t keep pace, making it difficult 
to move from AI pilot to production with confidence. HPE delivers AI 
solutions built for the realities of the enterprise, helping organizations 
progress from ambition to outcomes even when budgets, skills, and 
operational capacity are constrained. The HPE approach focuses on 
creating a strong, unified AI foundation designed for secure, sustainable, 
enterprise-scale deployment across hybrid environments. 

HPE leads with experience and expertise to guide the workload-focused 
decision points that drive a right-sized design—what must run now versus 
later, which workloads require acceleration, and how to operationalize AI 
without adding complexity. With an extensive portfolio of AI solutions and 
services that emphasize trusted data pipelines, AI-tuned operations, and 
turnkey hybrid platforms, HPE helps speed time to value while preserving 
flexibility and control, enabling organizations to turn AI initiatives into 
repeatable, measurable business outcomes.

Learn more at 

NVIDIA AI Computing 
by HPE

Visit HPE.com

A foundation for the future of AI

Whether pursuing AI inference and fine tuning, or data analytics, visual

toolu_01C8G4tx3D2jjUWRvkZawkVi.result:
I now have the complete text. Let me compile everything into a well-organized extraction.

Here is the complete technical content extracted from the document, organized by section:

---

## 1. Document Metadata

- **Title:** NVIDIA Enterprise AI Reference Architecture with HPE
- **Document Type:** Brochure
- **Document Number:** a00157780ENW
- **Copyright:** 2026 Hewlett Packard Enterprise Development LP
- **Publisher:** Hewlett Packard Enterprise (hpe.com)

---

## 2. Overview / What is this Architecture About

A jointly-validated reference architecture (RA) that fuses HPE compute, software, and storage with NVIDIA GPUs and software. It is designed to make it easier to deploy reliable, scalable, and high-performance AI solutions in enterprise data centers.

**Tagline / Positioning statement:** "Transforming data centers with flexible, scalable, and powerful server GPU solutions"

**Sub-headline:** "Redefining AI infrastructure with HPE and NVIDIA"

**Scale:** Validated and certified for 4–32 nodes (1–8 Scalable Units).

**Entry point:** RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition inside HPE ProLiant servers — an entry point into data center-scale AI, balancing performance, cost, and flexibility.

---

## 3. All Components

### Compute / Servers
- **HPE ProLiant Compute DL380a Gen12** — each server equipped with up to 8 RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
- GPU interface: PCIe-based (not NVLink/SXM — important positioning note)

### GPU
- **NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition** — up to 8 per server
- Fully validated at 256 GPUs total (32 servers × 8 GPUs)
- Supports: AI inference, fine-tuning, hybrid AI, HPC (single precision), visual computing, engineering simulation

### Networking
- **NVIDIA Spectrum-X Networking Platform** (specifically Spectrum-4)
- **NVIDIA ConnectX-7 SuperNICs and DPUs**
- Network topology: Rail-optimized architecture
- Supports custom rack layouts

### Software Stack
- **NVIDIA AI Enterprise** (subscription-based) — management, orchestration, AI inference tools
- **NVIDIA CUDA** — GPU compute framework
- **NVIDIA Magnum IO** — storage/IO acceleration

### Storage
- Not explicitly detailed with a specific product name in this document; mentioned as part of the broader HPE portfolio ("HPE compute, software, and storage platforms")

---

## 4. Architecture Design / Diagram Description (text-based)

**Scalable Unit (SU) model:**
- Base unit = 1 SU = 4 HPE ProLiant DL380a Gen12 servers
- Each SU = 4 servers × 8 GPUs = 32 RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs per SU
- Maximum configuration = 8 SUs = 32 servers = 256 GPUs
- Design is modular: start with 1 SU, expand to 8 SUs

**Network architecture:**
- Rail-optimized network layout
- NVIDIA Spectrum-4 switches
- ConnectX-7 SuperNICs and DPUs in each server
- Flexible support for custom rack layouts

**Management layer:**
- NVIDIA AI Enterprise (software management)
- Hardware support via HPE fulfillment
- Software support via NVIDIA AI Enterprise subscription

---

## 5. Use Cases Supported

1. **AI Inference** — for large models up to 70B parameters, powering copilots, assistants, and agentic AI
2. **AI Fine-Tuning** — models less than 70B parameters
3. **Hybrid AI** — mixed AI workloads
4. **Single Precision HPC** — high-performance computing for data processing and simulation
5. **Data Analytics** — accelerate insight and decision-making
6. **Visual Computing** — GPU-accelerated rendering and visualization
7. **Engineering Simulation** — accelerate design workflows
8. **Enterprise applications** — broad category requiring modular, scalable infrastructure

**Target customer profile:** Organizations seeking a flexible, scalable entry point into AI workloads at data center scale, prioritizing rapid deployment, reliability, and efficient scaling.

**Industries mentioned:** Healthcare & Life Sciences, Finance, Manufacturing, Retail

---

## 6. Sizing / Configuration Table

| Configuration | Servers (DL380a Gen12) | Scalable Units (SUs) | Total RTX PRO 6000 GPUs |
|---|---|---|---|
| Minimum | 4 | 1 | 32 |
| Mid | 8 | 2 | 64 |
| Mid | 12 | 3 | 96 |
| Mid | 16 | 4 | 128 |
| Mid | 20 | 5 | 160 |
| Mid | 24 | 6 | 192 |
| Mid | 28 | 7 | 224 |
| Maximum (fully certified) | 32 | 8 | 256 |

Note: The document states "scales from 4–32 nodes" and "32 certified systems for a total of 256 RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs in a fully tested system (8 SUs)."

---

## 7. Validated Software Versions

The document does not contain a detailed validated software version table (this is a brochure-level RA overview, not a full technical design guide). The validated software components named are:

- NVIDIA AI Enterprise (subscription)
- NVIDIA CUDA
- NVIDIA Magnum IO

No specific version numbers are cited in this document.

---

## 8. Presales / Positioning Messaging

### Key value propositions:
- **Time to insight:** "Speeds time to insight for AI projects of any size"
- **Jointly validated:** "Jointly-validated configurations" — reduces deployment risk
- **Modular / scalable:** Start with 1 SU (4 servers / 32 GPUs), expand to 8 SUs (32 servers / 256 GPUs) without redesigning
- **Entry-level cost:** RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (PCIe) positioned as a cost-effective entry point vs. NVLink/SXM systems
- **Dual support model:** Hardware from HPE, software from NVIDIA AI Enterprise subscription — clean accountability split
- **Ecosystem:** Joint HPE + NVIDIA engineering, not just co-marketing

### Common customer pain points addressed:
- Fragmented data
- Security concerns
- Infrastructure that can't keep pace with AI ambitions
- Difficulty moving from AI pilot to production
- Constrained budgets, skills, and operational capacity

### HPE differentiation claims:
- "Right-sized design" thinking — what must run now vs. later, which workloads need acceleration
- AI-tuned operations, trusted data pipelines, turnkey hybrid platforms
- "Turn AI initiatives into repeatable, measurable business outcomes"

### Product brand / portfolio name:
- **"NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE"** — the co-branded portfolio name for this collaboration

### Call to action (in the document):
- Visit: HPE.com / NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE page
- "Chat now" (live chat on HPE.com)

---

## Key Observations for Presales Use

1. This is a **brochure-level** document (not a deep technical design guide) — it establishes the portfolio and positioning but does not include rack diagrams, BOM tables, or validated software version matrices.
2. The **GPU is PCIe-based** (RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition) — this is explicitly not an H100/H200/GB200 NVLink-based system. It is positioned as an **enterprise entry point**, suitable for inference and fine-tuning of sub-70B models.
3. The **Spectrum-4 + ConnectX-7** networking is a key differentiator for scale-out performance.
4. The document was likely published in early 2026 (copyright 2026 HPE).
5. For a full technical deep-dive (validated BOM, port diagrams, software versions, rack layouts), you would need the full Technical Reference Architecture document — this brochure points to that as a companion piece.
